The world is more than 200,000 years old. Lots of animals have lived and expired over time. One of them was dinosaurs—they were huge and strong. Due to changes in the tectonic plates and environment, the Dinosaurs couldn't survive and disappeared. Now, you can see it in movies using computer-generated effects. However, cockroaches have been around for over 1,00,000 years and you can still come across them in your kitchen, living room, bathroom, trash can, and everywhere else. Cockroaches have adjusted to changes in their surroundings, which is why they still exist on the planet.

Even though companies produce items like HIT and Baygon to kill them, cockroaches survive by figuring out how to deal with those products. A study found that certain products cause some cockroaches to behave as if they're intoxicated. Their bodies adapt to these substances. This ability to change is known as adaptation.

There aren't any charities or groups dedicated to protecting cockroaches like there are for elephants and dolphins. Everyone wants to kill cockroach, yet these humble cockroaches keep on living and undefeated by human, and their existence is inevitable. Now that you understand, I trust you hold a greater appreciation for the modest cockroach. Similarly, Consultants must also adjust to shifts in market trends.

The IT market is going through changes like "Artificial Intelligence" and a higher demand for “SAP Full-Stack developers” and so on. In this blog we are go-through about SAP Full-Stack development.

(The story about cockroaches was inspired by a book called "11 Rules for Life" written by Chetan Bhagat)

II) Agenda:

  1. Getting to Know SAP FullStack: Understanding the Full Stack
  2. Background Insights: Delving into the Story Behind
  3. System Design Visualization: Seeing the Technical Architecture
  4. Practical Implementation: Learning the Practical Details Step-by-Step
  5. Wrapping Up: Summarizing Key Points and Looking Ahead
  6. Further Reading: Offering Links to More Research Materials

III) Getting to Know SAP Full-Stack: Understanding the SAP Full-Stack development

Many of our consultants believe that full stack development involves only front-end and back-end development. However, when it comes to SAP development, there's a third component: middleware.

In today's market, relying on just one system isn't enough. Data comes from various sources, and we need to consolidate it to generate reports. So, if you're interested in becoming an SAP full-stack developer, this blog will help you understand the basics of all three elements: front-end, middleware, and back-end, and explain them practically.

Fig1: Basic diagram for Full-Stack development

IV) Background Insights: Delving into the Story Behind

Sundhara Pandian is a big part of Kotravai Coffee Group in Queenstown, New Zealand. He makes really good coffee. His trick? He gets top-notch coffee beans by following a careful process.

Instead of just buying beans whenever he wants, Sundhara Pandian sends a request called a Purchase Order (PO) to a big coffee supplier in Bremen, Germany. But it's not as simple as filling out a form. The PO has to go through a smart system with AI and automation. This system checks the beans in the supplier's stock and confirms the order.

But Sundhara Pandian's job doesn't stop there. The results from the system are put into SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This helps keep track of orders and how much coffee is left.

Basically, Sundhara Pandian does more than just make coffee. He's good at handling  complicated systems to make sure Queenstown always has enough beans for coffee lovers.

Let's dive into SAP full-stack development through Sundara Pandian's story as we build the SAP RAP App for PO cockpit, iFlows and AI/ML model.

V) Technical Architecture Diagram: Visualizing the System Design

 

Fig2: Architecture diagram for E2E – Full Stack development with SAP applications

VI) Technical Implementation: Step-by-Step Integration Details

AIM: (Custom Cockpit and Integrated Goods Receipts):

  1. We're making a simple app for custom Purchase Orders (PO) with basic fields.
  2. Obtaining the GR information electronically, then utilizing a machine learning algorithm to automatically update the GR within our application system (GR automation).
  3. We're getting details about Goods Receipts (GR) from GR automation system and showing them in the custom PO form.

Target Audience:

  1. Individuals with 0 to 4 years of experience in SAP.
  2. Enthusiastic learners eager to explore new concepts and expand their knowledge base.

Before proceeding, please ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  1. Familiarity with basic concepts of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP RAP.
  2. Activation of the ADT-Eclipse, Postman, Integration Suite API plan in your SAP BTP entitlement.
  3. Understanding of fundamental AI and Automation concepts.
  4. Knowledge related to Python, Javascript, System landscape and UI

Step 1: Install the ADT package in the Eclipse. You can refer the below link to download the ADT package in the eclipse.

ADT Link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#abap

Step 2:

Step two has been divided into two sections, each detailed below:

  1. Section-A: Connect to the S/4 HANA Cloud system.
  2. Section-B: Create the modelling with SAP RAP

Go to help-> About Eclipse IDE -> Double check the below highlighted icon from your system.

Section-A: Connect to the S/4 HANA Cloud system

To connect the S/4 HANA Cloud system, please use the below steps.

Type the S/4 HANA Cloud web address: https://<Host>.s4hana.cloud.sap. Remember, select client 80. This client is used for making changes to the system.

Click the "Copy Logon URL to Clipboard" button. Then, open your web browser and paste the URL there. Enter your login details and press enter. After successful login, you'll see the screen below.

Click "Finish" and create your own package name. I've used "ZDEMO" here.

Part 1: Key Takeaway:

  1. Gaining foundational knowledge of systems and landscapes through story-based learning.
  2. Grasping the fundamentals of full-stack development with diagrams
  3. Setting up Eclipse for SAP ADT and establishing a connection to the S/4 HANA Public Cloud system.

Part 2: Coming soon - Keep an eye out for an exciting story-based learning journey as we delve into creating SAP RAP applications.

Part 3: Coming soon - Embark on an exploration of AI and ML with our forthcoming model, create system with a dashboard for ( GR Automation ) 

Part 4: Coming soon - Learn how to connect the circuit using Integration Suite in our upcoming installment.

Author: If you find this information helpful, please consider clicking the "Like" button on this blog and sharing your thoughts in the comments section below. You can also connect with the author on their LinkedIn profile: [Author's LinkedIn Profile]

entry.title: Why YCOA? The value of the standard Chart of Accounts in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
entry.content:
Would you buy a brand-new Electric Vehicle and pull out the motor to replace it with your old petrol engine? Of course not. The electric motor is the heart of the vehicle and designed to deliver the best outcome in terms of efficiency, performance and user experience.

So how is this relevant to SAP? The Chart of Accounts (“CoA”) is the heart of Finance and a well-designed and well-structured CoA will enable you to get the optimum performance from your S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

In my experience (>25 years and literally 100’s of customers) with SAP Financials, I’ve seen that accountants are extremely attached to their General Ledger (“GL”) accounts. They know the GL account numbers and the postings within them. I’m aware there is a desire to retain that knowledge and the history it represents. This article is to explain some of the benefits of adopting the standard settings out of the box and possibly make you think again about Renumbering the delivered GL accounts.

To be absolutely clear, I’m not proposing that you adopt the delivered YCOA  content without any changes. It’s expected that you would need to:

  • Create new GL accounts
  • Flag accounts as “Not used”
  • Change the Text

 

A little background on YCOA

I’ll assume that you are familiar with the concepts of the CoA within S/4HANA, including the types (Operating, Alternate and Group) as well as the data structure across the CoA, Company Code and Controlling Area levels.

Your system will include:

  • YCOA as the standard Operating CoA
  • YGR1 as the Consolidation (Group) Chart of Accounts if you have activated Scope Item 3AF Group Account Preparation for Financial Consolidation
  • YIKR or other Country specific Alternative CoA, delivered for specific countries
  • Additional Alternative CoA you have created

Illustration 1. List of Chart of Accounts

Whilst you can change the Description and the General Specifications, you can’t change the name YCOA and you can’t change the assignment to the Company Codes as the Operating Chart of Accounts.

Illustration 2. CoA settings maintenance

Illustration 3. Assignment of YCOA as the Operating CoA

Operating Chart of Accounts YCOA comprises:

  • the “header” information with the name, description and parameters for the attached GL accounts (as above)
  • The GL accounts within the CoA, which are defined at the CoA, Company Code and Controlling levels as required

The structure of GL account numbers is Industry independent, noting that there is also a variant delivered for US Public Sector.

Illustration 4. Standard YCOA structure

The Operating CoA contains the GL accounts which are maintained in configuration for Finance processes and integration with other modules. They are the primary set of GL accounts used for posting transactions, planning and reporting.

So whilst YCOA is fixed as the Operational CoA, it is possible to Renumber (and rename) the individual delivered GL accounts (as well as excluding from transport to Test and Prod systems).

SAP Help for Chart of Accounts: Different Types

SAP Help for Renumbering

SAP Help for Implementation Status

 

Renumbering

Why Renumber?

Why do customers Renumber? The most likely response is to make the CoA appear as similar as possible to the legacy system. There may also be a desire to align with a group, country or other statutory standard reporting structure.

There are certainly benefits, including:

  • Reduced change impact on users
  • Simpler mapping and reconciliation in data conversion
  • Retain integration to external systems which use GL numbers
  • Aligns to historical reporting and planning data

This may seem compelling and could be difficult to push back on, particularly if the option to Renumber has been positioned in the Discover phase. After all, if it’s supported by SAP, it must be ok...right?

Well, yes and no…as with many things in life, just because something is possible doesn’t always make it a good idea!

Note that in the following, “Renumbering” is mainly focussed on changing the GL account numbers (and overall structures) although technically the process can also include Renaming GL accounts, as well as setting to “Not used”.

Also as mentioned above, when I refer to working with the delivered CoA, this does include creation of new GL accounts to meet your requirements but within the existing account number ranges.

Renumbering is easy?

Firstly, I’d point out that Renumbering is not a quick, simple exercise to do properly.

Whilst SAP delivers tools to make the system changes for the renumbering, there is still a significant effort required to:

  • Understand the intended purpose of the delivered GLs, their settings and how they are used in the standard Best Practise processes as well as Account Determination configuration
  • Identify which GL accounts are to be retained and renumbered
  • Define new numbers and names
  • Map the SAP delivered GL account numbers to new (legacy) numbers
  • Execute across the landscape

All of this takes time and effort to understand, communicate and agree with the customer. This is particularly the case for new GL Accounts in YCOA with no equivalent to Legacy processes such as those for Event Based Revenue Recognition. The customer needs to have a solid understanding of the new process to be able to engage with the mapping and Renumbering process.

Also bear in mind that an account can only be renumbered if it has not been posted against in Test or Production (in a 3 system landscape). This means you need to execute the renumbering early in the project at the same time other project activities are in progress.

You will also need to find a suitable window for the conversion step, avoiding time when SAP upgrades are running and you will need users to be off the system.

All of this is contrary to the Activate methodology and Cloud Mindset. An S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project is supposed to be about the adoption of standard solution and process. The intention is to go-live as quickly as possible to start realising the benefits from the system. This also means avoiding activities which are not absolutely necessary and which could delay the project go live.

Renumbering is not a one-off activity

Just because you have run the Renumbering once, it doesn’t mean the process is done with.

Additional General Ledger Accounts are automatically created in YCOA with the activation of new Best Practise Scope Items and additional Countries. These GL accounts will be created with the standard SAP GL numbering.

You will need to need to be identify these accounts and Renumber again prior to transport and first posting in Test or Production. This also means that for a new process which you may not be completely familiar with, you’ll need to work out how the postings fit into the customer’s CoA structure. The subsequent projects to extend scope become longer and more complex.

Dependent settings after Renumbering

The Renumbering process will update GL master data, configuration (including Account Determination) and transactional data in the Development system (as no transactions can be posted on the changed GL accounts in Test or Production).

However, there are further aspects which will need to be reviewed, particularly as Renumbering may well impact the structure and grouping of the accounts which is part of the standard YCOA. Accounts which were previously part of a logical range may now be in very different number ranges. This means any previous assignments based on groups or ranges needs to be checked, including:

  • Financial statement versions, particularly any assignments based around number ranges rather than individual GL accounts
  • Mapping of FSV items to semantic tags
  • Cost Element groups

Note also that some of the Semantic Tags and FSV are hard coded for use in specific processes, such as:

  • Tag FX for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
  • Tag ACT_COST for Availability Control for Projects
  • FSV YPS2 for Margin Analysis reporting – refer Microlearning Why YPS2 Financial Statement Version is Important in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Cost Element group YBPS_BILLI for Intercompany Billing

The reality is that not every dependency is obvious or well documented. The more you diverge from the delivered settings, the more you risk hitting an unexpected issue.

Mapping challenges – additional GL accounts

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a fundamentally different solution with different processes and architecture to both previous SAP solutions such as ECC and from 3rd party vendors. It follows that the GL accounts used in these processes will not be the same and any mapping for Renumbering will highlight these differences.

An existing SAP customer coming onto S/4HANA will usually find there are a range of differences requiring new GL accounts due to the differences in process and posting, for example:

  • Revenue Accounts previously managed as Value Fields in Costing Based COPA
  • Accounts for COGS split previously only requiring Value Fields in Costing Based COPA
  • Accounts for Variance Categories previously only requiring Value Fields in Costing Based COPA
  • Accounts for Statistical Revenue items
  • Event Based posting GL accounts particularly for Revenue Recognition
  • Accounts for Secondary Cost Elements

Mapping challenges – reduced GL accounts

Some GL accounts from the legacy CoA are not required in standard YCOA, for example:

  • Bank Reconciliation GL accounts where several House Banks can post to a single GL
  • Reconciliation Account Adjustment accounts as Advanced Valuation can post directly to the original account
  • Accounts used in Accounts Approach for Parallel Accounting which is replaced by the Ledger Approach

OK so now you understand some of the additional aspects of Renumbering to match an existing CoA, let’s discuss some of the benefits you get with standard delivered YCOA.

 

Benefits of YCOA

Comprehensive documentation of YCOA

One of the hidden gems of YCOA is the detailed documentation behind both the structure of the account numbering and also the individual GL accounts.

https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/ycoa/list-of-accounts

Illustration 5. YCOA documentation

The SAP documentation describes:

  • The technical attributes of GL accounts
  • The Account Structure of how GL account mapping to Financial Statements reporting
  • GL account usage in Best Practise scope items
  • GL account usage in Account Determination
  • Best Practice Process usage and posting examples

It also includes the accounts used for additional scope items such as Joint Venture Accounting and Contract Accounting, as well as the additional GL accounts created for specific Countries.

Each GL account is described in detail in terms of its purpose and how it is used in business transactions, as well as the related accounts with similar usage.

The Account Determination information is particularly useful, with information on the automated postings from integrated processes from MM and SD as well as Finance transactions and Tax related items across supported Countries. Much of this information is not available elsewhere and this also means you don’t need to maintain a complete accounting guide for your users to explain the account usage.

This documentation is updated for every release of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, meaning it is always current and contains details for new scope items, including Country specific items.

All of this is based on the delivered GL account numbers in YCOA and would become redundant with a renumbered CoA.

BP accelerators

In addition to the Help documentation, there are Excel files available in Signavio Process Navigator in SAP for ME:

Preconfigured G/L account master data for YCOA (Global)

This provides the complete list of GL accounts at the CoA level and whether they are included in Company Code level for the delivered Country versions.

It also includes GL accounts created from non standard Scope Items and as Country Specific, along with details of GL usage in Account Determination.

Preconfigured G/L account master data for YCOA (Local) - Country specific

This contains the GL account information for a specific Country.

Best Practise Test Scripts

InSignavio Process Navigator, each Best Practise Scope Item includes a Test Script which typically covers:

  • Set up information including relevant configuration settings
  • Test steps including inputs and expected results

Only the delivered standard GL accounts are mentioned in these documents.

Whilst the Renumbering process will update the configuration in the system, there is no equivalent process to update the Test Scripts. If you or your customer are using them to understand the standard process, you will need to map any GL accounts to their new values.

Test Automation

You can also load the Best Practice scope items into the Test Automation tool.

How to perform automated testing using standard best practice automates in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool? Part-1

Data used for the Test Process is managed with the Test Containers, including the relevant, the GL account numbers to be used in the test. When running the standard processes, the system will use the delivered YCOA GL account numbers. This reduces the effort to set up the test process and means they should execute correctly.

The Renumbering process does not automatically update the Test Containers. You will need to manually identify where GL accounts are used and then map to the new number.

Baseline Activation Service

The SAP Baseline Activation Service is an implementation accelerator which delivers a Fixed Scope of processes and Org Structure objects to kick start the project. This Service, delivered by SAP, provides a rapid foundation for the overall implementation delivered by the Partner. Partners are also able to develop and deliver their own implementation accelerators.

The Baseline Activation Service assumes that the standard processes and the YCOA settings are used out of the box. The Service includes Transports and execution of Processes in the Test system (which prevents subsequent Renumbering of the posted GL accounts). The expectation is that the implementation project will extend this configuration and not need to start again with a revision of the Chart of Accounts or retest the processes in scope of the service with new GL accounts.

Other SAP documentation

GL account numbers are mentioned across a wide range of documents and resources including:

  • SAP Help
  • SAP Learning content
  • Blogs
  • Detailed configuration guides and Cookbooks for example for EBRR
  • Notes and KBA, for example

    2965577 - SAP Best Practices Content for ICMR for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Edition

    This note has files which document all the delivered Inter Company Matching and Reconciliation configuration loaded with Scope items 40Y and 1SG, based around YCOA standard accounts.

All reference material from SAP will use the standard YCOA GL account numbers, which makes it simpler to reference to your own environment. This consistency makes it easier for consultants and users to move across SAP systems and it also makes it easier to learn new functions based on the common GL references.

Group Reporting

SAP solution Group Reporting uses Financial Statement Items (“FSI”) in the Consolidation process. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, these FSI are mapped from the posted GL account number.

Illustration 6. Mapping GL account to Financial Statement Item

The FSI represents a more summarised view of the financial transactions and hence there is usually an N:1 relationship between the GL account and the FSI. The FSI are usually less digits than the GL accounts.

In the standard system, there is a logical alignment between the standard YCOA GL account numbers and the FSI, with a common first digit and often a common second digit. This makes it simpler to understand the relationship between the values in the GL and Group Reporting.

If you Renumber the GL accounts in YCOA, you will also need to determine whether to renumber the FSI. There is no equivalent automated Renumbering process so you would need to create the FSI and define the mapping from the GL accounts.

Broader Benefits

I would also argue that there are benefits in adopting a standard GL structure for both Customers and Partners beyond those listed above.

  • In an era of staff shortages and higher mobility, onboarding is easier where the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is maintained as close to standard as possible.
  • Skills and employees become more transferable rather than being constrained by the customer specific view of GL accounting and reporting
  • Support time and expense is reduced when working with a common consistent set of GL account numbers for each implementation, rather than each being a unique set up.
  • There is more opportunity for Partners to build and deploy automation tools and accelerators based on the delivered YCOA. This provides opportunity to add value for Customers , reduce costs and improve implementation outcomes.

 

YCOA enables transformation

YCOA is delivered as an integral part of a modern accounting platform supporting processes which were not available in legacy environments. It represents SAP’s collective experience in delivering Financial processes, with rigour, logic and structure.

The delivered GL accounts are “natural” accounts without reference to internal or external elements such as customers, products or channels. They work hand in hand with the broader SAP Organisation structures (Company Code, Profit Center, Cost Center etc) and Margin Analysis to provide a Customer and Product view. Where additional dimensions are required, these can be achieved with simple Extensibility solutions rather than through the creation of additional GL accounts.

Legacy systems and their CoA were not designed for a modern ERP or to support capabilities such as:

  • Ledgers supporting multiple Accounting Principles
  • Ledger supporting parallel valuations (Legal, Group)
  • Extension Ledgers for additional adjustments
  • Predictive and statistical Ledgers
  • Fully integrated subledgers (Bank, AR, AP, Assets, Material Ledger) in the Universal Journal
  • Fully integrated Margin Analysis
  • Event Based Revenue Recognition
  • Event Based Production Accounting for WIP and Variance
  • Advanced Valuations for GL closing

Moreover YCOA is constantly updated by SAP as new processes and Countries are released.

These enable new ways of managing the business and provide an opportunity to reimagine the end-to-end reporting processes with new metrics and KPIs.

I would also go as far as to propose that Renumbering and the use of customer specific GL Account numbers provides no distinct competitive advantage and just retains the legacy view of processes. Where required, the equivalent reporting outcomes can be achieved by retaining and extending the YCOA numbering.

Whilst this may add to the up-front business change impact, it will clearly signal that the new system is bringing transformation. It also means that the ongoing adoption of new functions, one of the pillars of the Cloud Mindset, can be enabled in a seamless manner.

 

In conclusion

It might be stating the obvious, but transformation (of processes and business outcomes) requires changes which can be challenging. However, expecting transformation without change is like sticking butterfly wings on a caterpillar and hoping it will fly!

Convinced? If nothing else, make sure your customers are aware of these impacts before taking their Electric Vehicle back to the Fossil Fuel age.

entry.title: How Defense and Security Organizations can Easily Migrate to the Cloud with RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Figure 1: Example picture

 

1) Introduction

­­

In the recent years, cloud computing has been among the most important IT trends, and it will continue to do so within the next decade. The leading technology consulting firm, Gartner, published various insightful statistics about the technology and its importance that highlight the importance for both private enterprises and public organizations. For one thing, as the below graph shows, the market for public cloud services will grow to $1 trillion by 2026. This relates to a growth rate of more than 100% when compared to the year 2021 and even marks the highest growth rate of all existing IT markets.

 

Figure 2: Forecast for public cloud services growth by Gartner

 

This strong trend towards cloud computing is also reflected in a Gartner survey among 367 enterprises . As you can see in the chart below, more than 50% of the survey participants in 2022 think that cloud technology is either indispensable or heavily impactful. When asked about the year 2027, even 90% believe that this will be the case.

Figure 3: Gartner cloud computing survey

 

Furthermore, 50% of surveyed organizations will use industry cloud platforms to accelerate their digital business initiatives in 2027, according to Gartner. In contrast, less than 5% of the participants gave this answer in 2022.

 

2) The Importance of Cloud Computing for the Defense and Security Industry

 

Cloud services are also of high importance for Defense and Security (D&S) organizations, like armed forces, aid organizations or emergency organizations. A recent study financed by the European Defense Agency shows that cloud computing can be applied to both the strategic level and the field level of military organizations. Furthermore, cloud technology can support European armed forces in achieving information superiority against its adversaries. The advantages of cloud technology apply to emergency organizations, like police forces, in a similar manner. Here cloud technology does not only improve the collaboration within and across police organizations, but also helps to complete criminal investigations faster. Fire departments can benefit from the technology as well. Examples for this include cost reductions, access to innovative IT services, a higher reliability of IT services or improved cybersecurity. Rescue service organizations, in turn, can profit from cloud computing through a better and faster handling of medical data. In this way, rescue chains in general and the planning of medical treatments can be improved significantly, which in the end can help to save people’s lives.

 

3) What is RISE with SAP?

 

Figure 4: Introductory video to RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP is a holistic package for customers, which want to transform their existing on-premises SAP systems into the cloud. RISE with SAP thereby not only includes SAP’s top-of-the-range software solution, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, but also various connected services, such as, cloud infrastructure and security as well as related migration and modernization programs. Businesses and organizations, which are not yet SAP customers can instead use GROW with SAP. GROW with SAP is an offer, that is specifically designed for new customers and small and midsized companies and organizations. But let us now take a closer look what specifically RISE with SAP offers for existing customers.

 

Figure 5: RISE with SAP overview graphic

 

The RISE package generally makes system transformations easier and allows customers to profit from all of the latest innovations from SAP. The key components of RISE – as the above graphic shows – are:

1) Cloud Solutions

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition allows you to optimize processes in an unprecedented way. The Cloud ERP solution in this regard covers all common business functions of Defense and Security organizations like, for instance, procurement, human resource management or military planning and operations. Additionally, RISE with SAP includes packages, like the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for app development, data management, automation and real-time analytics and SAP Signavio for process mining. The RISE solution beyond this also includes diverse innovative and helpful SAP Business Artificial Intelligence functions and features, that allow your organization to get better insights and save valuable time and money. What is more, the RISE program enables D&S organizations to use our holistic sustainability software, SAP Sustainability Solutions. These solutions not only allow organizations to monitor and manage sustainability and environmental issues, but also to ensure the safety of their employees at their workplaces.

 

2) Cloud Infrastructure and Security

RISE with SAP is built on the foundation of state-of-the-art IT infrastructure and security. In this regard, our customers can benefit from a very modern cloud infrastructure, that helps to ensure the highest possible system performance and availability of services around the clock. Furthermore, D&S organizations can profit from simple and effective service level agreements (SLAs), that guarantee a 99.7% availability as well as regular maintenance of our SAP cloud solutions. Moreover, our RISE with SAP offering is based on the highest standards for security and data protection. This not only entails regular security updates, but also the ability of permanently monitoring the system with regards to performance, compliance and potential threats.

 

3) Migration and Modernization

Our RISE with SAP cloud package aims to make the migration from on-premises systems to modern cloud systems as simple as possible. SAP for this created an easy, guided implementation process, that is based on the SAP Activate methodology. This methodology allows our customers to:

  • Gain an in-depth understanding of the extensive capabilities of cloud ERP.
  • Receive expert guidance to strategically plan and prepare for a smooth migration.
  • Discover new avenues to craft detailed implementation plans across multiple workstreams.
  • Seamlessly progress through all six implementation stages with clarity and precision.
  • Tap into a vast partner ecosystem for enhanced support and collaboration.
  • Access comprehensive assistance, that is available in more than 100 countries worldwide.

 

4) The Key Advantages of RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP unites all advantages of cloud computing technology with the well-known advantages of SAP software solutions. The combination of the two technologies thereby even enabled SAP to create its most advanced ERP products so far, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The main advantages of RISE with SAP are:

High scalability and usage-based pricing

Our cloud solutions allow your D&S organization to easily scale your SAP systems, in a needs-based manner. This is not limited to a certain part of your organization, but can be applied across your entire global operations. The high scalability thereby not only facilitates your organization’s IT processes, but also pays off financially due to the “pay only what you need approach”.

 

Better collaboration

SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows for a better collaboration among your workforce. Your employees are enabled to work jointly on tasks, communicate directly and share information. This can be done for instance with the SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, a versatile tool for process collaboration. Furthermore, our cloud ERP solutions also enable our clients to better collaborate with their stakeholders, such as suppliers.

 

Process automatization and access to diverse innovative functions and features

The RISE with SAP offering entails access to various innovative platforms, functions, and features as well as to a variety of useful partner solutions. Examples for this are the SAP Business Technology Platform or SAP Business Artificial Intelligence, two solutions, that were designed to complement each other perfectly. The BTP, on the one hand, is a platform, that bundles services like data management and analytics, application development, automation, and integration as well as artificial intelligence in one environment. The platform is thereby also helping to accelerate the transformation to the cloud significantly. SAP Business AI, on the other hand, is an umbrella term for all available AI solutions from SAP. Good examples for this are SAP Joule, a Generative Artificial Intelligence-based copilot within SAP applications. SAP Joule can answer diverse questions, guide the user through the application or even solve various tasks in an automated manner. Other examples for SAP Business AI include the automatization of processes, such as guided buying or automatically generated job descriptions.

 

5) Migration Options

 

D&S organizations, which are already using SAP solutions and want to migrate their systems to the cloud can choose among two principal migration options:

1) Migrate from SAP ERP

D&S organizations, that use SAP ERP and want to migrate to the state-of-the-art SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition can profit from a guided transformation process. This process allows the organizations to make use of diverse best practices for the migration of their systems and data throughout all phases of the transformation journey. Principal advantages of this concept are that customers are enabled to maintain system configurations just like their data sets easily in the new cloud environment.

 

2) Migrate from SAP S/4HANA

The migration from an existing on-premises SAP S/4HANA system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is even less complex. The transition to the cloud here works very frictionless, which means that – in most cases – customers can just transform their systems and data centers to the new S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition environment.

 

3) Alternative for new customers: GROW with SAP

As already mentioned above, SAP recommends new customers the tailored cloud transformation program GROW with SAP. GROW with SAP allows for a fast and predictable implementation of the SAP cloud systems and includes most of the advantages of the RISE offering. Unlike the RISE with SAP package, the GROW solution relies on a Public Cloud architecture in the form of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

6) Security Features of RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP is built on a comprehensive security architecture, that enables our customers not only to work safely, but also to protect their data regardless from where and in which local circumstance they work. The security architecture comprises enterprise-grade security features and standards, like:

  • Physical access control
  • User authentication and authorization functions
  • Advanced functions for the function and user-based separation of data
  • Data encryption
  • Automated back-ups and protection from data losses (data recovery)
  • Data encryption features
  • Full compliance with data protection guidelines like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)

- Comprehensive protection of client data

- Auditing functions

- Integrated capabilities for deleting personal data

  • Sovereign Cloud solutions for organizations with higher security and data-protection requirements, including:

- Use of data centers, that are located in the resident country or region of the respective company or organization

- Data being solely stored and processed within the sovereign borders of the country or region

- Corresponding proof of national and international security certifications and attestations

- Adherence to high security standards throughout all steps

- Increased security requirements for employees

 

7) Cloud Solutions with Advanced Security Features by SAP NS2

 

For companies and organizations, which have even higher cybersecurity requirements, SAP is offering cloud solutions with advanced cyber security measures. SAP therefore established its own independent subsidiary called SAP National Security Services (SAP NS2). SAP NS2 is mainly focused on the US, Canadian and Australian market, where it, among other things, is responsible for the adherence of SAP products to diverse security and regulatory requirements. The focus of SAP NS2 thereby lies on ultra-secure cloud solutions for highly regulated industries like D&S organizations, but also for actors with such needs within other industries. This includes highest level of security standards, like the US Department of Defense Impact Level 4 (DoD IL4) requirements just like the US Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) certification. The company’s product portfolio includes:

  1. SAP Secure Cloud Services and Products
    • SAP NS2 offers a great range of SAP cloud products and solutions with enhanced security standards. The product portfolio includes ERP-solutions, like SAP S/4HANA, as well as diverse solutions for functional areas, like Supply Chain Management, Finance, Human Resource Management or Spend Management. In addition, NS2, with the SAP Business Technology Platform, also offers diverse automation, analytics, data management and Artificial Intelligence solutions.        
  2. Professional Services
    • SAP NS2 offers its customers a variety of Professional Services, that range from cloud transformation over advisory services to implementation services.                                                      
  3. Support
    • SAP NS2 permanently supports its customers with a highly specialized customer service team, which answers any kinds of questions or issue resolution.                                                                   
  4. Mission Solutions 
    • SAP NS2 offers a variety of additional services under its brand DataCTRL, that allow D&S customers to securely manage and catalog data across their organization. DataCTRL thereby enables D&S organizations to perform a better and faster data handling, which, for instance, can speed up the migration from on-premises to cloud systems.

 

Key Takeaways

 

All in all, this blog entry has shown that RISE with SAP brings significant benefits for Defense and Security organizations, that shouldn’t be ignored. This includes advantages, such as, process automatization just like better collaboration or access to the latest functions and features. What the blog has also shown is that cloud technology, like RISE with SAP, can make all types of D&S organizations more efficient and successful, and here even allows them to strengthen themselves within their core competencies. Although the security of cloud solutions is typically a rather controversial issue among IT responsibles, it has been shown in the blog that SAP’s cloud solutions are built on the foundation of a strong security architecture. This architecture includes various security functions and features like automated backups, encryption, or access control functions. For organizations with even higher cybersecurity requirements, SAP can refer its customers to its specialized subsidiary, SAP NS2. SAP NS2 offers ultra-secure cloud solutions with the highest possible security certifications for highly regulated industries, like Defense and Security. Beyond that, our customers can expect numerous further innovations for the future from SAP, which includes Generative AI functions just like the stepwise integration of SAP’s intelligent copilot, Joule, into SAP applications.  

 

Product Tour for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

 

If you are interested in learning more about RISE with SAP and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and its advantages for the different operational processes in the following product tour:

Figure 6: Link to the RISE with SAP product tour

 

Sources

 

Accelerate Digital Transformation, Enable Compliance, and Mitigate Risk with Sovereign Cloud Capabilities from SAP. (2023, September). SAP.

Breaking the cloud barrier in aerospace and defense. (2022). Accenture.

‘Combat cloud’: EDA study shows benefits of cloud computing for EU militaries. (2024, January 24). European Defense Agency.

Digitalisation of Defence in NATO and the EU: Making European Defence Fit for the Digital Age. (2023, August 10). IISS.

Healthcare Cloud Computing: Meaning, Advantages, and Implementation Pitfalls. (2023, August 16). IEEE.

How fire departments can extinguish money, space, and data loss with the cloud. (2020, May 11). ZDNET.

Military Cloud Computing Market. (2023, September). MarketsandMarkets.

RISE with SAP Overview. (2024). SAP.

SAP National Security Services Subsidiary. (2024). SAP NS2.

The Future of Cloud Computing in 2027. (2022). Gartner.

Transition to SAP S/4HANA. (2024). SAP.

Why using the cloud is about more than just efficient storage. (2022, September 2). POLICE1.
